questions.question: What is the correct syntax for pseudo-elements?
questions.explanation: Pseudo-elements should use double colon syntax (::before, ::after). Single colon is for pseudo-classes.

questions.question: Which property is required for ::before and ::after to work?
questions.explanation: The content property is required for ::before and ::after pseudo-elements. Without it, they won't appear.

questions.question: What does ::first-letter do?
options: Styles the first letter of a block element
Creates a first-letter drop cap automatically
questions.explanation: ::first-letter applies styles to the first letter of a block-level element's text content.

questions.question: Can ::before and ::after be used on void elements like ?
options: No, they don't work on void elements
questions.explanation: Pseudo-elements don't work on void elements (img, input, br, hr, etc.) because they have no content or closing tag.

questions.question: What is used to add quotation marks around a blockquote using pseudo-elements?
questions.explanation: Use ::before { content: '“'; } and ::after { content: '”'; } to add decorative quotation marks.

questions.question: What does ::selection allow you to style?
options: The selected option in a dropdown
questions.explanation: ::selection styles the portion of text that a user has highlighted or selected with their mouse or keyboard.

questions.question: What properties can be styled with ::first-letter?
options: Font, color, margin, padding, border, and background
questions.explanation: ::first-letter supports font properties, color, margin, padding, border, background, and a few others. Not all CSS properties are supported.

questions.question: Which pseudo-element styles the bullet or number of a list item?
questions.explanation: ::marker styles the marker box of a list item, which contains the bullet (ul) or number (ol).

questions.question: Is content inserted via ::before read by screen readers?
options: Yes, content added via CSS is read
questions.explanation: Content generated by the content property on ::before and ::after IS announced by screen readers.

questions.question: What is the difference between ::first-letter and ::first-line?
options: ::first-letter styles the first character; ::first-line styles the first line
questions.explanation: ::first-letter targets only the first character, while ::first-line targets all text on the first line (which changes with viewport width).

questions.question: What value should content have for purely decorative pseudo-elements?
questions.explanation: For decorative elements that don't add text, use an empty string: content: ''. This creates the element visually without adding readable content.

questions.question: Can pseudo-elements be combined with pseudo-classes?
questions.explanation: Pseudo-elements and pseudo-classes can be combined: .element:hover::after applies the pseudo-element only when the element is hovered.

questions.question: What does ::placeholder allow you to style?
options: The placeholder text in an input
questions.explanation: ::placeholder targets the placeholder text inside input and textarea elements.

questions.question: Which pseudo-element can be used to create a tooltip?
questions.explanation: ::before or ::after combined with positioning (absolute/relative) is commonly used to create CSS-only tooltips.

questions.question: How can you create a clearfix using pseudo-elements?
options: Pseudo-elements can't be used for clearfix
questions.explanation: The standard clearfix technique uses ::after with content, display: table, and clear: both to contain floated children.

questions.question: What happens when you set content: attr(data-tooltip) on a pseudo-element?
options: It copies the data-tooltip attribute value
questions.explanation: The attr() function in content retrieves the value of an HTML attribute, allowing dynamic content in pseudo-elements.

questions.question: Can ::first-letter be used on inline elements?
options: No, only on block or inline-block elements
questions.explanation: ::first-letter only works on block-level or inline-block elements. It doesn't work on pure inline elements like <span>.

questions.question: What does ::backdrop pseudo-element style?
options: The background of a full-screen element
questions.explanation: ::backdrop styles the background of elements shown in full-screen mode (e.g., dialog:fullscreen, video:fullscreen).

questions.question: Which is a valid way to prevent pseudo-element content from being read by screen readers?
questions.explanation: Adding aria-hidden='true' to the parent element (not the pseudo-element) tells screen readers to ignore the element and its generated content.

questions.question: What does ::slotted() do in CSS?
options: Creates a slot in the layout
Styles content inserted into a Shadow DOM slot
Removes an element from the DOM
questions.explanation: ::slotted() is used in Shadow DOM to select content that is inserted into a <slot> element.
